[Now, no design system is complete without my personal favorite - controls.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=670) [Controls have an all new look in macOS Tahoe.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=676) [The new design creates a stronger family resemblance across devices,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=680) [unifying the appearance of elements like buttons,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=684) [switches and sliders between macOS, iOS and iPadOS.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=687) [These changes have been thoughtfully applied to retain the character](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=692) [and capability that you expect from Mac controls.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=695)







11:40







[macOS controls are available in a variety of standard sizes,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=700) [ranging from mini all the way up to large.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=704) [These sizes establish varying levels of density](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=708) [and hierarchy among your controls.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=711) [macOS Tahoe adds one more size to this list -](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=714) [extra large - for emphasizing your most important actions.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=717)







12:02







[The extra large size is ideal](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=722) [for showcasing the most prominent actions in your application.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=724) [These are the actions that people launch your app to get done,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=728) [like queuing up some music in a media player](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=731) [or placing a call in a communications app.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=733) [In addition to the new size,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=737) [we’ve also taken the opportunity to rethink the heights of controls.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=738)







12:23







[Compared to previous releases of macOS,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=743) [the mini, small, and medium controls are now slightly taller,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=746) [providing a little more breathing room around the control label](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=750) [and enhancing the size of the click target.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=753) [To adapt to varying control heights,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=757) [use Auto Layout and avoid hard-coding the heights of controls.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=759) [For compatibility with existing high-density layouts,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=763) [like complex inspectors and popovers,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=767) [AppKit provides an API to request control sizes](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=770) [that match previous releases of macOS.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=773)







12:56







[Use the new prefersCompactControlSizeMetrics](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=776) [property on NSView.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=779) [This property is inherited down the view hierarchy,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=782) [and when it's set to true,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=785) [AppKit controls will revert to sizing that is compatible](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=787) [with previous releases of macOS.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=790)







13:13







[The new design system introduces some new control shapes as well.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=793) [The mini through medium sizes retain a rounded-rectangle shape,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=797) [which enables greater horizontal density,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=800) [while the large and extra-large sizes round out into a capsule shape,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=803) [making use of all that extra space.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=808) [To achieve concentricity in your custom designs,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=811) [you can override the preferred shape of a control.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=814)







13:38







[In this example, I’ve built a custom call-out bar](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=818) [for spell-checking using medium-sized controls.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=821) [The container for the bar has a capsule shape](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=824) [but it doesn’t fit well with the rounded rectangle controls inside.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=827)







13:52







[This is a perfect use case for the new borderShape property.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=832) [This API allows you to override the shapes of buttons,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=836) [pop-up buttons and segmented controls.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=839)







14:03







[By overriding these controls to use a capsule shape,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=843) [they fit nicely within my custom container.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=846)







14:09







[In addition to the shape, you can also customize](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=849) [the material of a button using the new glass bezel style.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=852)







14:16







[This bezel style replaces the standard button backing](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=856) [with the Liquid Glass material,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=859) [which is perfect for buttons that need to float atop other content.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=861) [The glass bezel style is compatible with the existing bezelColor property,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=865) [which tints the glass using the provided color.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=869)







14:34







[The new design system also introduces the idea of control prominence to AppKit.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=874) [By varying the prominence of a button, you can control the level of visual weight](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=879) [given to its tint color.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=883) [This allows you to add color to a button without upstaging higher-prominence](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=885) [controls inside the same interface, such as the default button.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=889) [This technique is used for destructive buttons.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=894) [The distinctive red color is a helpful hint that the action is destructive](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=897) [but with a level of prominence that doesn’t overpower nearby controls.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=902)







15:07







[The tint prominence type has four cases: automatic,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=907) [which indicates that the control should choose a level](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=910) [of prominence appropriate for its style and configuration;](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=913) [none, which indicates minimal or no tint color;](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=917) [secondary, which indicates a more subdued application of the tint color;](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=921) [and primary, which applies the tint at the most prominent level.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=926)







15:32







[To apply a lower prominence tint to a button,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=932) [set the tintProminence property to secondary.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=935) [By default, this will display using the accent color.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=938)







15:42







[In this example, I’m treating the Play button a little differently](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=942) [because I want it to behave as the default button,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=946) [so I’ve given it the return key equivalent.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=949) [This ensures that the button responds to the keyboard in a predictable way,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=952) [and since it’s the default button, it’ll automatically apply](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=956) [the most prominent level of tint.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=958)







16:03







[Tint prominence also has a function with sliders.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=963)







16:06







[The tintProminence API allows you to choose whether the track](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=966) [is filled with the accent color.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=969) [A slider set to none will avoid filling its track,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=972) [whereas a slider set to secondary or primary will fill it.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=975)







16:20







[The slider fill has learned one more trick in macOS Tahoe.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=980) [It can anchor itself at any location along the track,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=984) [rather than just the minimum end.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=987) [Use the new neutralValue property](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=990) [to set a value that serves as the anchor for the track fill.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=992)







16:36







[In this example of a playback speed control,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=996) [I’ve set the neutralValue to 1x, so that when the speed is made slower](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=999) [or faster, the blue fill helps communicate the difference](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1003) [between the selected value and the default value.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1007) [The new design system also brings an update to menus](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1011) [with a refreshed appearance and a significant expansion](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1014) [in the use of icons.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1017)







17:01







[Both menu bar menus and context menus now use icons](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1021) [to represent their key actions.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1024)







17:07







[Within each section of a menu,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1027) [the icons form a single column that’s easy to scan through.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1029) [Adding clear, recognizable symbols to your menu items helps people quickly](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1033) [find the most important actions in the menu.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1037)







17:21







[The “Get to know the new design system” video provides a ton of additional guidance](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1041) [for choosing symbols for your menu items.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1045) [Be sure to check it out.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1048) [Finally, integrating Liquid Glass elements into your app.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1050)







17:34







[Before you integrate the Liquid Glass material into your custom UI elements,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1054) [it’s important to think about the design intent behind this new material.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1059)







17:43







[Liquid Glass elements float at the top level of the UI,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1063) [elevating the controls and navigation into a distinct functional layer.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1067)







17:52







[With that in mind, limit your use of Liquid Glass](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1072) [to the most important elements in your app,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1075) [the controls that belong in this top level of hierarchy.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1077) [Freeform’s inline editing controls are a great example.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1081) [They float above the content rather than sitting alongside,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1084) [and they work beautifully with the Liquid Glass material.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1088)







18:13







[To place your content on glass, use the NSGlassEffectView API.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1093) [Setting a contentView allows AppKit to apply all of the necessary visual](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1097) [treatments to keep your content legible as the glass adapts to its surroundings.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1101)







18:26







[So avoid placing the NSGlassEffectView behind your content as a sibling view.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1106)







18:32







[You can customize the appearance of the glass using the corner radius](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1112) [and tint color properties.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1115) [I’ll take you through an example of adopting NSGlassEffectView](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1118) [for an existing element.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1121)







18:44







[In this example, I have a fitness app which shows daily training stats](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1124) [and a custom control for picking the type of workout.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1129) [I’m displaying them using a horizontal NSStackView.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1133) [Now, this is a prominent part of my UI,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1136) [so I’m going to put both parts of it on glass.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1139)







19:02







[Adopting the Liquid Glass material takes just a few new lines of code.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1142)







19:07







[First, create an NSGlassEffectView for each glass element](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1147) [that you want to display](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1150) [and set each one’s contentView property to the desired view.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1152) [The glass effect view ties its geometry to the contentView using Auto Layout,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1157) [so you don’t have to worry about keeping them in sync.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1162)







19:26







[Then, put the glass effect views into the view hierarchy.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1166) [In this example, I updated the stack view to swap in the new glass effect views.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1170)







19:35







[If you have multiple glass shapes in close proximity,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1175) [group them together using NSGlassEffectContainerView.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1179) [The glass effect container view combines multiple glass elements together](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1183) [into a single rendering effect.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1187) [This has a few benefits.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1189)







19:51







[First, grouped glass elements can fluidly join](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1191) [and separate using a liquid visual effect.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1194) [The glass shapes meld together based on their proximity](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1198) [and the value of the spacing property,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1201) [which is available on NSGlassEffectContainerView.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1204)







20:08







[Second, the adaptive appearance of the glass](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1208) [is shared across the grouped elements,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1211) [which ensures that they maintain a uniform appearance](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1213) [as the underlying content changes.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1215)







20:19







[And grouping is important for visual correctness.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1219) [The Liquid Glass material reflects and refracts light,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1223) [picking color from nearby content.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1227)







20:30







[To create this effect,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1230) [the glass material samples content from an area larger than itself.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1231) [But what happens if that sampling region includes another glass element?](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1236) [Well, glass can’t directly sample other glass,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1240) [so the visual results in this case will not be consistent.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1243)







20:47







[Using a glass effect container allows these elements](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1247) [to share their sampling region.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1250) [Not only does this provide a more consistent visual result](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1252) [but it also improves the performance of the glass effect,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1255) [since it only needs one sampling pass for the entire group.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1258)







21:03







[Revisiting the sample from earlier,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1263) [these two glass effects are part of a logical group,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1265) [so they need to be inside a glass effect container.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1268) [It’s straightforward to set one up.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1271) [In this example, I create an NSGlassEffectContainerView](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1273) [and set the stack view as its content view.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1277) [The container and its content view are also constrained together](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1280) [using Auto Layout,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1284) [So I can cleanly swap this container into my existing layout.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1285) [The Liquid Glass material is a powerful tool for elevating your app’s key controls](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1289) [and enabling your content to flow seamlessly from edge to edge.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1294) [It’s a great way to highlight the functionality that makes](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1298) [your app unique.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1300)







21:43







[So what's next?](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1303) [As a first step, build your app with Xcode 26.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1304) [A lot of the new design will start working right away.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1308) [Extend your content edge-to-edge wherever possible,](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1312) [taking full advantage of the floating glass toolbar and sidebar.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1315)







22:00







[Then, adapt to the new control sizes by auditing your app](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1320) [for hard-coded control heights or inflexible layout constraints.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1323) [Enhance your menu actions with symbol icons](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1328) [and identify key elements of your interface to elevate](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1331) [with the Liquid Glass material.](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1334)

- - 0:00 - [Introduction](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=0)
  - Learn about the updated design system for macOS. The new design introduces Liquid Glass, a translucent surface that adds depth and dynamism to the user interface.

    AppKit is now updated to support this new design, including changes to basic structural components, the introduction of a scroll edge effect for legibility, and updates to the appearance and layout of controls. Learn how to implement these changes and utilize new APIs to incorporate Liquid Glass into your custom UI elements.

  - 1:23 - [App structure](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=83)
  - The new Mac design system introduces Liquid Glass for toolbars and sidebars, enhancing hierarchy and visual appeal. Toolbar elements are automatically grouped on Liquid Glass, which adapts its appearance based on content brightness. Toolbar items can be customized.

    Sidebars appear as floating glass panes, while inspectors use edge-to-edge glass.

    The design system emphasizes concentricity, with softer window corner radii that vary based on style. To position content near corners, the new 'NSView.LayoutRegion' API provides corner-avoiding layout guides, ensuring a polished and elegant user interface.

  - 9:27 - [Scroll edge effect](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=567)
  - The new design introduces the scroll edge effect, a visual separation between edge-to-edge content and floating Liquid Glass elements. This effect manifests as either a soft fade or a hard opaque backing and dynamically adapts within 'NSScrollView' as floating elements change. The effect is automatically applied underneath toolbar items, titlebar accessories, and new split item accessories, which span one split within a split view controller and which you can place at the top or bottom edge. These accessories influence the effect's size and shape, insetting the content safe area for easier layout.

  - 11:10 - [Controls](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=670)
  - In macOS Tahoe, the control design system is updated to enhance consistency across Apple devices. The new system includes an extra-large control size for emphasizing primary actions, and slightly taller mini, small, and medium controls for better readability.

    Control shapes now vary based on size; smaller sizes are rounded rectangles, while larger ones are capsules. You can customize control shapes, materials, and prominence using the new APIs. The tint prominence property allows controls to have varying levels of visual weight, making it easier to distinguish between different actions.

    Sliders are also updated, with the ability to anchor the fill at any location and to use tint prominence.

    Menus are refreshed with a more modern appearance and now prominently feature icons in a single column for easier scanning and navigation.

  - 17:30 - [Glass](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1050)
  - To integrate Liquid Glass elements into your app, consider the design intent and limit its use to the most important controls that float at the top level of the UI hierarchy.

    The 'NSGlassEffectView' API provides functionality to place content on glass, and you can customize its appearance with corner radius and tint color properties. When multiple glass shapes are close together, group them by using 'NSGlassEffectContainerView' to ensure fluid visual effects, uniform appearance, and consistent sampling of nearby content.

  - 21:30 - [Next steps](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2025/310/?time=1290)
  - To leverage the latest design features, build your apps with Xcode 26. This allows for immediate implementation of edge-to-edge content, utilizing the floating glass toolbar and sidebar. Enhance menu actions with symbol icons and incorporating Liquid Glass material for key interface elements to modernize your app's look and feel.

![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/E2nQsw0El8M/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [Mastering Liquid Glass in SwiftUI – Buttons, Containers & Transitions](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=E2nQsw0El8M)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Stewart Lynch](http://www.youtube.com/@StewartLynch)
**Uploaded at**: 2025-08-31T06:22:14-07:00
**Published at**: 2025-08-31T06:22:14-07:00
**Length**: 26:51
**Views**: 21677
**Likes**: 788
**Category**: Science & Technology

## Description

```
SwiftUI’s Liquid Glass effect in OS 26 is more than just eye candy—it’s a whole new design system. In this tutorial, I’ll guide you through the new button styles, applying glass effects to various views, and using GlassEffectContainer to create seamless, fluid layouts.

We’ll also explore namespace-powered unions, matched geometry transitions, and clever control interactions that elevate your app’s UI. This is hands-on, practical, and comprehensive.

Whether you’re building for iOS, macOS, or visionOS, Liquid Glass is a game-changer—and I’ll help you get the most out of it.

If you want to support my work, you can buy me a coffee :)
```
